entities.name: NITRIC OXIDE
entities.description: A crucial signaling molecule that protects plants from stressful conditions through various biological mechanisms.

entities.name: PLANTS
entities.description: The biological organisms being studied for their stress tolerance mediated by nitric oxide.

entities.name: STRESSFUL CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental factors that trigger stress responses in plants.

entities.name: STRESS RESPONSES
entities.description: The biological reactions of plants to stressful conditions.

entities.name: ANTIOXIDANT DEFENSE
entities.description: A protective mechanism activated by nitric oxide to combat stress.

entities.name: METABOLIC HOMEOSTASIS
entities.description: The maintenance of a stable internal environment within the plant, supported by nitric oxide.

entities.name: GENE TRANSCRIPTION
entities.description: The process of copying DNA into RNA, which is altered by nitric oxide during stress responses.

entities.name: POSTTRANSLATIONAL PROTEIN MODIFICATIONS
entities.description: Chemical modifications of proteins after translation, altered by nitric oxide.

entities.name: EXOGENOUS DONORS AND SCAVENGERS
entities.description: Chemical tools used to manipulate endogenous nitric oxide levels for research.

entities.name: MUTANTS
entities.description: Organisms with genetic mutations used to explore the functions of nitric oxide.

entities.name: TRANSGENICS
entities.description: Genetically modified organisms used to explore the functions of nitric oxide.

entities.name: SIGNALING PATHWAYS
entities.description: The underlying series of chemical reactions in a cell that lead to a specific response to nitric oxide.

entities.name: CROSSTALK EVENTS
entities.description: The interaction between nitric oxide and other signaling molecules under stress responses.

entities.name: SIGNALING MOLECULES
entities.description: Molecules that transmit signals to cells, including nitric oxide and others involved in crosstalk.

entities.name: GENETIC AND MOLECULAR APPROACHES
entities.description: Scientific techniques used to elucidate the production and signaling cascade of nitric oxide.

entities.name: NO RESPONSIVE GENES
entities.description: Genes that respond to the presence or levels of nitric oxide under stress.

entities.name: PROTEINS
entities.description: Biological molecules and networks that are responsive to nitric oxide under stress.
